titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 1321
GA 1321
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Recording of first hand and correction at 5.3-4 changed by David Parker after consultation with Rachel Kevern.
First hand and corrector tags added at 5.3-4.
Word break at 4.27 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Iota adscripts removed by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Catherine Smith.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number:1321 Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed:242rBase text used: TRns Transcribed by:Eirini Panou Transcription begun: July 1 2010Transcription finished: July 28 2010 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - Yes Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (>) - Yes: on the following folios: 279v, 280r. Obeloi (†) - Yes: on the following folios: 284r. Other remarks: Iota adscript is used, though inconsistently. Letters and words obscured by ink or damage. Few pages difficult to read because of microfilm’s bad quality (257bv, 258r, 263v,264r )

ab.note: The scribe originally finished F253v at the end of line 18 with εν ταυταις. There are two and a half lines of text erased at the top of F254r where he probably continued and completed verse 3. He replaced what he had written in the two and a half line space at the top of F254r with three and a half lines. We cannot be certain what text the scribe first wrote and then erased but it is most likely that he omitted verse 4 and began copying verse 5
